title: Neonauclea parviflora
intro: Neonauclea parviflora, also known as the "Malay tea tree" or " Malabar tree," is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae that is native to Southeast Asia. The leaves of Neonauclea parviflora have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its potential health benefits of reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Neonauclea parviflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal preparations, Neonauclea parviflora is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into tinctures and ointments, to leverage its bioactive constituents and provide potential therapeutic benefits.

The genus Neonauclea is a part of the tribe Naucleae, which includes other species of flowering plants with similar characteristics.
